expectations.text: Chooses a hook (not a skill) because the user wants automatic enforcement
expectations.evidence: Agent summary confirms PreToolUse hook approach chosen; response explicitly justifies automatic enforcement

expectations.text: Chooses PreToolUse event (not PostToolUse) because it must block before execution
expectations.evidence: Agent summary confirms PreToolUse chosen; response explains blocking must happen before execution

expectations.text: Provides a shell script that checks git push commands for main/master patterns
expectations.evidence: block-main-push.sh written with git push pattern matching for main/master

expectations.text: Uses non-zero exit (exit 1 or exit 2) to block the tool call
expectations.evidence: Agent summary confirms exit 2 used with blocking message

expectations.text: Includes exit 0 for non-matching commands to avoid accidental blocking
expectations.evidence: Agent summary confirms 'Exit 0 allows everything else through'
